maritime.title: NERAI Maritime Weekly
events: Tanker Struck and Set Ablaze Off Saudi Coast in Red Sea on 23 July
Strait of Hormuz Transits Hit Near-Standstill as US-Iran Conflict Enters 12th Day
Houthi Blockade Forces Saudi Tankers to Reverse Course, Asian Refiners Reroute via Suez
Gulf Producers Race to Activate Hormuz Bypass Routes as Pipeline Alternatives Gain Urgency
Somali Pirates Board Tanker Off Yemen on 17 July Amid Regional Security Vacuum
maritime.summary: This week's maritime landscape was dominated by the active US-Iran conflict and its cascading effects across two critical chokepoints simultaneously: ongoing vessel strikes near the Strait of Hormuz depressed transits to severe levels, while Houthi forces escalated by threatening and attacking Saudi oil tankers in the Red Sea, forcing rerouting via the Cape of Good Hope and Suez Canal. The convergence of a near-standstill at Hormuz and a credible Houthi blockade threat at Bab el-Mandeb represents the most serious dual-chokepoint energy shipping crisis in recent memory, driving oil prices to six-week highs above $96 and prompting Goldman Sachs to warn Brent could exceed $120 if the crisis deepens.

sanctions.title: NERAI Sanctions Weekly
events: EU 21st Russia Sanctions Package Stalls; Oil Price Cap Locked In Amid LNG Surge
U.S. Cuba Sanctions Tightened; Meliá Exits as Spy Accusations Escalate
South Korea Seizes Cargo Vessel Suspected of Violating UN North Korea Sanctions
Houthis Announce Maritime Blockade of Saudi Arabia Ports
sanctions.summary: This week's sanctions landscape was dominated by three converging pressures: a sustained U.S. campaign against Iranian procurement and shipping networks, turbulence inside the EU's Russia sanctions architecture as the 21st package stalled over energy and member-state interests, and an intensifying U.S. squeeze on Cuba that is already producing measurable third-party commercial exits. Secondary developments — including a South Korean vessel seizure for North Korea sanctions violations and a Danish shipyard controversy over Russian gas-tanker maintenance — underscore that enforcement is broadening across multiple regimes simultaneously.
